_note: Per-lesson role-play scenario. Grounded in B1/002 dialogue + vocab. Learner plays Anna; computer plays Tom. Each NPC node offers 2-3 branches so the same lesson language can be reached different ways. Consumed by RP.Engine + RP.classifiers.local (see roleplay/).
title: 002 · Planning a Trip

npc.text: If the weather is nice, we will go to the mountains.

routes.meaning: Anna is excited and asks what they should bring.
examples: That sounds great! What should we bring?
Sounds great! What should we bring?
Cool! What do we need to bring?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “That sounds great! What should we bring?”

routes.meaning: Anna asks more about the mountains.
examples: What can we do in the mountains?
routes.hint: You can ask “Which mountains will we visit?” or “What should we bring?”

partial.message: Ask Tom about the trip: “That sounds great! What should we bring?”

off_topic.message: Tom has a plan! Say “That sounds great! What should we bring?”

npc.text: We can hike the trails near the green hills.

routes.meaning: Anna asks what they should bring.
examples: That sounds great! What should we bring?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “That sounds great! What should we bring?”

partial.message: Ask “That sounds great! What should we bring?”

npc.text: We should bring water, snacks, and a map.

routes.meaning: Anna asks how they will get there.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “How will we get there?”

examples: We should also bring a jacket.
routes.hint: You can add an idea, or ask “How will we get there?”

partial.message: Ask about travel: “How will we get there?”

off_topic.message: Ask Tom “How will we get there?”

npc.text: Good thinking! Let's pack a jacket too.

routes.meaning: Anna asks how they will get there.
examples: So how will we get there?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “How will we get there?”

off_topic.message: Ask Tom “How will we get there?”

npc.text: If we leave early, we can take the morning train.

routes.meaning: Anna asks what they will do if it rains.
examples: What will we do if it rains?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “What will we do if it rains?”

routes.meaning: Anna asks what time they should leave.
routes.hint: You can ask “What time should we leave?” or “What will we do if it rains?”

partial.message: Ask about bad weather: “What will we do if it rains?”

off_topic.message: Ask Tom “What will we do if it rains?”

npc.text: We should leave at seven, before the station gets busy.

routes.meaning: Anna asks what they will do if it rains.
examples: What will we do if it rains?
But what will we do if it rains?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “What will we do if it rains?”

partial.message: Ask “What will we do if it rains?”

off_topic.message: Ask Tom “What will we do if it rains?”

npc.text: If it rains, we will visit a museum instead.

routes.meaning: Anna agrees and says they should plan carefully.
examples: Good idea. We should plan carefully.
Good idea! We should plan carefully.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Good idea. We should plan carefully.”
You can ask “Which museum will we visit?” or say “Good idea. We should plan carefully.”

partial.message: Agree with Tom: “Good idea. We should plan carefully.”

off_topic.message: Tom has a rainy-day plan. Say “Good idea. We should plan carefully.”

npc.text: The city museum has a big dinosaur exhibit.

routes.meaning: Anna agrees and says they should plan carefully.
examples: Good idea. We should plan carefully.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Good idea. We should plan carefully.”

partial.message: Say “Good idea. We should plan carefully.”

npc.text: I think it will be an exciting trip.

routes.meaning: Anna can't wait for the trip.
Anna is a little nervous about the trip.
examples: I hope we don't forget anything.
routes.hint: You can say “I can't wait!” or “I hope everything goes well.”

off_topic.message: Tom is excited. Say “I can't wait!”

npc.text: Me neither! Let's start packing tonight.
Don't worry, we planned it well. It will be fun!
